steps.title: Read what Sumner actually did
steps.content: The sentence describes Sumner as someone who helped found the psychology department at Howard University, directly increasing opportunities for Black students to study psychology. He was an active creator of that opportunity, not a passive recipient or minor contributor.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and find the match
steps.content:
Consider every option:
  • "proponent of": A proponent actively advocates and champions a cause. Founding a department to expand access is exactly that kind of active support. ✓
  • "supplement to": This implies Sumner was secondary or add-on to something else; the passage presents him as a primary actor, not an afterthought. ✗
  • "beneficiary of": A beneficiary receives advantages from something rather than creating them; Sumner was building opportunity for others, not receiving it. ✗
  • "distraction for": A distraction hinders a cause; Sumner was advancing it. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
